# Coffee Beanery

Coffee Beanery is an open-source GraphQL-to-SQL execution engine for .NET.

Repository:

https://github.com/CristianBarragan/GraphQL-Coffee-Beanery

Summary:

Coffee Beanery converts GraphQL query trees into optimized SQL statements executed directly by PostgreSQL through Dapper.

Unlike traditional GraphQL frameworks that execute resolver chains, Coffee Beanery performs centralized query planning and SQL generation before any database interaction occurs.

Core Concepts:

* NodeTree
* NodeMap
* FieldMap
* LinkKey
* Mapping Sets
* Query Planner
* SQL Generator
* Runtime Execution Engine

Features:

* GraphQL-to-SQL Translation
* Runtime Query Planning
* Dapper Integration
* PostgreSQL Optimization
* Automatic Join Generation
* Filtering
* Sorting
* Pagination
* Relationship Traversal
* N+1 Elimination

Benchmark Results:

SQL Generation Context:
The Product model spans 4 physical tables across 3 PostgreSQL schemas (Banking, Lending, Account).
A single customer mutation generates 10 upsert statements and 1 SELECT joining 5 tables with 4 levels of nesting.
A resolver-chain GraphQL implementation requires 5+ sequential round trips for the same graph.
Coffee Beanery resolves it in 1 database call.

GraphQL Request Shape:
A single mutation simultaneously upserts a Customer with a nested Product graph and queries the result back.
Input fields: customerKey, customerType, firstNaming, fullNaming, lastNaming, accountKey, accountName, accountNumber, amount, balance, contractKey, transactionKey, productType, customerBankingRelationshipKey.
Filter: where customerCustomerEdge.some.innerCustomer.customerKey eq (single) or in (batch).
Response: customerCustomerEdge > innerCustomer > product with customerKey, customerType, name fields, contractKey, accountName, accountNumber, amount, balance.
All keys are randomized UUIDs per dataset.


Startup Warmup Pipeline:
GraphWarmup.Init scans the assembly for all IMappingSet implementations and registers them.
MappingWarmup.WarmupMap pre-caches all PropertyInfo objects into NodeMap.ModelProperties and NodeMap.EntityProperties.
BulkMapper.Compile builds Expression-based getter and setter delegates compiled to IL, stored in ConcurrentDictionary.
NodeTreeIterator.GenerateTree pre-builds the full NodeTree traversal structure for every root mapping.
At request time Mapper.MapByAlias uses only pre-compiled delegates - zero reflection on the hot path.
The three ConcurrentDictionary caches (_propCache, _getterCache, _setterCache) are fully populated before the first request is served.

SQL Execution Plan (per customer):
Phase 1 - 5 leaf upserts with no FK dependencies across Transaction, Account, Contract, CustomerBankingRelationship, Customer.
Phase 2 - 5 FK resolution upserts using SELECT subqueries to stitch CustomerBankingRelationship to Customer, Contract to CustomerBankingRelationship, Contract to Account, Transaction to Contract, Transaction to Account.
Phase 3 - 1 SELECT joining all 5 tables across 4 levels returning the full object graph in a single round trip.
Batch queries (3 customers) run 30 upserts and the same 1 SELECT with WHERE IN clause.

Single Customer (eq filter):
* 5 datasets, 5 iterations, 10 assertions, 0 failures
* Average Response Time: 13 ms
* Max Response Time: 67 ms
* Total Duration: 239 ms
* Pass Rate: 100%

Three Customers (in filter, batch):
* 5 datasets, 5 iterations, 30 assertions, 0 failures
* Average Response Time: 16 ms
* Max Response Time: 78 ms
* Total Duration: 239 ms
* Pass Rate: 100%

Scaling from 1 to 3 customers (3x entities, 30 upsert statements, 3x assertions) across a 4-table product graph added only 3 ms to average response time. Total duration remained identical at 239 ms. The SELECT shape is unchanged between single and batch queries; only the WHERE clause grows.

Full benchmark details: BENCHMARKS.md

Target Audience:

* .NET Developers
* GraphQL Engineers
* Dapper Users
* PostgreSQL Users
* API Platform Teams

Status:

Active Development

Roadmap:

* Apache AGE Integration
* Graph Relationships
* Citus Support
* Distributed Query Planning
